datasets.name: mecdm_superapp_db
datasets.description: PostgreSQL/AlloyDB dataset for the Meghalaya Early Childhood Development Mission (MECDM) SuperApp. Contains 25 tables across six categories: (1) PostGIS geographic boundaries at state/district/block/village level; (2) canonical master-reference tables keyed by LGD codes; (3) health infrastructure with GPS points; (4) maternal-child health tracking (mother_journeys is the central fact table, keyed by pregnancy_id — one row per pregnancy, so a mother with multiple pregnancies has multiple rows with the same mother_id); (5) raw API JSON records normalized into relational tables; (6) survey and reference data (NFHS, videos, research). Geography hierarchy: states → districts (12) → blocks (46) → villages (~6,862). Health hierarchy: mother_journeys → anc_visits (via mother_id); mother_journeys → raw_pregnancy_records (via pregnancy_id FK). Use LGD codes (district_code_lgd, block_code_lgd, village_code_lgd) to join health data to geographic tables. Spatial queries use PostGIS — geometry column on GeoJSON-sourced tables, geom column on CSV-sourced point tables (health_facilities, anganwadi_centres). All geometries are EPSG:4326 (WGS84).

tables.name: mother_journeys
tables.description: Central fact table. One row per pregnancy (NOT per mother). A mother with multiple pregnancies has multiple rows sharing the same mother_id. Some rows are child-only (no pregnancy registration) and have NULL pregnancy_id. Covers registration, all ANC visit summaries, delivery outcome, and child outcome in a single denormalised row. ~363,898 rows.
tables.notes: row_id is SERIAL surrogate PK. mother_id is non-unique. pregnancy_id is UNIQUE with NULLs allowed (Postgres UNIQUE allows multiple NULLs). pregnancy_id format: '{mother_id} - {sno}'. gps_location stores WKT string — use ST_GeomFromText(gps_location, 4326) to convert.

tables.name: anc_visits
tables.description: Individual Antenatal Care visit records with clinical measurements. ~361,551 rows. Join to mother_journeys via mother_id.
tables.notes: Join to mother_journeys: anc_visits.mother_id = mother_journeys.mother_id (logical join, not enforced FK — mother_id is non-unique in mother_journeys).

tables.name: village_indicators_monthly
tables.description: Monthly aggregated maternal and child health indicators at village level. ~133,071 rows. Key table for time-series analytics.
tables.notes: year_month format: 'YYYY-MM'. Join to master_blocks via block_code_lgd.

tables.name: raw_pregnancy_records
tables.description: Raw API JSON response records for pregnancy journeys, normalized to relational columns. ~363K rows. Source of truth for all pregnancy fields. FK to mother_journeys via pregnancy_id.
tables.notes: pregnancy_id FK → mother_journeys.pregnancy_id (enforced). Contains all raw API fields not present in mother_journeys CSV.

tables.name: raw_anc_records
tables.description: Raw API JSON response records for individual ANC visits, normalized to relational columns. ~361,508 rows.
tables.notes: pregnancy_id column may be absent (not in source API). Join to mother_journeys via mother_id.

tables.name: raw_child_records
tables.description: Raw API JSON response records for child/infant data, normalized to relational columns.
tables.notes: pregnancy_id column may be absent. Join to mother_journeys via mother_id.

tables.name: states
tables.description: Meghalaya state polygon (1 feature). PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'. Contains Census 2011 state-level demographic attributes.
tables.notes: Spatial queries: ST_Within, ST_Intersects etc. Use SRID 4326.

tables.name: districts
tables.description: Meghalaya district polygons (12 features) with Census 2011 demographics (rural/urban split, workers, SC/ST populations). PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'.
tables.notes: Join to master_districts for LGD codes.

tables.name: district_boundaries
tables.description: Meghalaya district boundary polygons using 2021 delineation (12 features) with area_sqkm and length attributes. PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'.
tables.notes: Use for spatial containment queries with 2021 boundaries.

tables.name: subdistricts
tables.description: Sub-district (block) polygons (46 features) with Census demographics and agricultural data. PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'.
tables.notes: Corresponds to blocks in health data. Join via block name.

tables.name: blocks
tables.description: Block boundary polygons (46 features) with area and length measurements. PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'.
tables.notes: Administrative blocks — the primary sub-district unit in MECDM data.

tables.name: villages_poly
tables.description: Village boundary polygons for ~6,862 Meghalaya villages with Census 2011 demographics. PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'.
tables.notes: Use for spatial aggregations. Join to master_villages via village name or LGD code.

tables.name: villages_point
tables.description: Village representative GPS points for ~6,862 villages with Census 2011 data. PostGIS geometry column: 'geometry'.
tables.notes: Use for point-in-polygon queries (e.g. which block does a village fall in).

tables.name: master_districts
tables.description: Canonical district reference table with LGD (Local Government Directory) codes. 12 districts.
tables.notes: Reference for normalizing district_name strings in health tables.

tables.name: master_blocks
tables.description: Canonical block reference with LGD codes and parent district. 47 blocks.
tables.notes: FK: district_code_lgd → master_districts.

tables.name: master_villages
tables.description: Village reference with LGD matching status (~7,430 villages). match_confidence is 'exact' or 'unmatched'.
tables.notes: village_id format: 'V-NNNNN'. Many villages are 'unmatched' for LGD codes.

tables.name: master_health_facilities
tables.description: Health facility reference: PHCs, Sub-centres, and Other Public Facilities (~1,799 facilities).
tables.notes: facility_id format: 'HF-NNNNN'. facility_type values: PHC, Sub-Center, Other Public Facility.

tables.name: geo_full_mapping
tables.description: Complete geographic hierarchy mapping: district → block → facility → village with LGD match confidence.
tables.notes: Use for hierarchical aggregation queries across all levels.

tables.name: geo_match_report
tables.description: Quality report for village-to-LGD code matching.
tables.notes: Diagnostic table — shows which villages could not be matched to LGD codes.

tables.name: health_facilities
tables.description: Health facility directory enriched with GPS coordinates. PostGIS POINT in 'geom' column. ~646 facilities.
tables.notes: geom is PostGIS POINT(lon lat) EPSG:4326. Spatial join with geographic tables to find nearest facility.

tables.name: anganwadi_centres
tables.description: ICDS Anganwadi Centre directory with GPS, infrastructure (water, toilet, building type), and administrative data (~5,900 centres). PostGIS POINT in 'geom' column.
tables.notes: geom is PostGIS POINT EPSG:4326. geometry_x = longitude, geometry_y = latitude (duplicate columns).

tables.name: meghealth_geo_mapping
tables.description: Facility-to-village mapping used in Meghealth: district → block → PHC → sub-centre → village hierarchy.
tables.notes: Text-based mapping — join to master tables via name or LGD code for numeric keys.

tables.name: nfhs_indicators
tables.description: National Family Health Survey (NFHS) rounds 3/4/5 district-level health indicators. 624 records.
tables.notes: nfhs_round values: 3 (2005-06), 4 (2015-16), 5 (2019-21). Compare rounds to show trend.

tables.name: video_library
tables.description: Educational video metadata for ASHA/ANM health workers (~49 videos on breastfeeding, nutrition, immunization etc).
tables.notes: tags, health_indicators, target_audience stored as JSON strings. category values: BREASTFEEDING, NUTRITION, IMMUNIZATION etc.

tables.name: research_articles
tables.description: Peer-reviewed research articles on maternal and child health in Meghalaya/Northeast India. 23 articles.
tables.notes: key_findings and relevant_indicators stored as JSON strings.

foreign_keys.note: DB-enforced FK. pregnancy_id has a UNIQUE constraint (NULLs allowed) in mother_journeys; the actual PK is row_id (SERIAL). References mother_journeys(pregnancy_id) ON DELETE CASCADE.
DB-enforced FK (conditional — only added if pregnancy_id column exists in raw_anc_records). ON DELETE CASCADE. pregnancy_id column may be absent depending on source API response.
DB-enforced FK (conditional — only added if pregnancy_id column exists in raw_child_records). ON DELETE CASCADE. pregnancy_id column may be absent depending on source API response.

Logical join — not a DB FK because mother_id is non-unique in mother_journeys (one row per pregnancy, not per mother). Use: anc_visits.mother_id = mother_journeys.mother_id. To get all ANC visits for a specific pregnancy, join via pregnancy_id through the shared mother_id.
Logical join via mother_id. pregnancy_id column may be absent in raw_anc_records depending on source data.
Logical join via mother_id. pregnancy_id column may be absent in raw_child_records depending on source data.
Logical hierarchy: blocks belong to districts via LGD code.
Logical hierarchy: villages belong to blocks via LGD code.
Join monthly indicators to geographic hierarchy via LGD block code.
